questions.question_text: Under Rea 701.03, which detail must be disclosed about a property's water supply?
questions.explanation: Rea 701.03 requires disclosure of the water supply type, location, any malfunctions, installation date, and the most recent test date. Monthly bill amounts and pressure readings are not specified requirements.

questions.question_text: A seller has a private well installed in 2010. Under Rea 701.03, which information must be disclosed?
distractors: Only the well depth and flow
questions.explanation: Rea 701.03 requires disclosure of the water supply type, location, any malfunctions, installation date, and most recent test date. Well depth and flow rate, while useful, are not the specific statutory requirements.

questions.question_text: Under Rea 701.05, what must a seller disclose about the property's sewage disposal system?
questions.correct_answer: Tank size, type, location, and age
questions.explanation: Rea 701.05 requires disclosure of the septic tank size, system type, location, any malfunctions, and the age of the system. Last pumping date alone would not satisfy the disclosure requirement.

questions.question_text: A seller knows the septic system has had repeated backup issues. Under Rea 701.05, must this be disclosed?
distractors: Only if the buyer specifically asks
Only if system is over 20 years old
questions.explanation: Rea 701.05 specifically requires disclosure of any malfunctions of the sewage disposal system. Repeated backup issues are malfunctions that must be disclosed regardless of the system's age or whether the buyer asks.

questions.question_text: Under Rea 701.04, what must a seller disclose about insulation?
questions.explanation: Rea 701.04 requires disclosure of the insulation type and extent if known to the seller. There is no requirement to provide R-values, cost, brand names, or installer information.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 477:4-a, when must environmental hazard disclosures be provided to the buyer?
questions.correct_answer: Before the purchase and sale agreement
distractors: At the closing table during final signing
Within 10 days after the closing date
Only when the buyer formally requests them
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-a requires that the seller notify the buyer about environmental hazards such as radon, arsenic, lead paint, and PFAS before the purchase and sale agreement is executed.

questions.question_text: A 1965 home is listed for sale. Under RSA 477:4-a, which hazard must the seller specifically disclose?
questions.explanation: Under RSA 477:4-a, sellers must notify buyers about lead-based paint for homes built before 1978. A 1965 home falls within this pre-1978 threshold, triggering the lead paint disclosure requirement.

questions.question_text: Which environmental hazard was added to NH seller disclosures in January 2025 via HB 398?
questions.explanation: HB 398, effective January 2025, added PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) to the list of environmental hazards that must be disclosed under RSA 477:4-a. Radon, arsenic, and lead were already required.

questions.question_text: Which substances must a NH seller disclose under RSA 477:4-a before signing the purchase and sale agreement?
distractors: Only radon and lead paint hazards
Only PFAS and mold spore exposure
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-a requires disclosure of radon, arsenic, lead-based paint (pre-1978), and PFAS (added January 2025 via HB 398). All four must be disclosed before the P&S agreement.

questions.question_text: A seller fails to provide environmental disclosures and the buyer signs the P&S agreement. What is the status of this transaction?
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-a requires environmental hazard disclosures before the purchase and sale agreement. Failure to provide them is a violation of this statute regardless of whether the buyer signed the P&S.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 477:4-d, effective July 2024, what must a seller disclose about a 1-4 family property?
questions.correct_answer: Whether it is in a FEMA flood zone
distractors: The property's elevation above sea level
History of all water damage claims
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-d, effective July 2024, requires sellers of 1-4 family residential properties to disclose whether the property is located in a FEMA-designated flood hazard zone.

questions.question_text: A seller lists a single-family home in a FEMA flood hazard zone. Under RSA 477:4-d, when must flood zone disclosure occur?
questions.correct_answer: Before the purchase and sale agreement
distractors: At the closing appointment during final signing
Within 30 days of listing the property for sale
Only if the buyer formally requests this information
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-d requires the seller to disclose flood zone status to the buyer before the purchase and sale agreement. This applies to all 1-4 family residential properties in FEMA flood hazard zones.

questions.question_text: RSA 477:4-d flood zone disclosure applies to which property types?
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-d specifically applies to 1-4 family residential properties. Commercial properties and those with five or more units are not covered by this particular flood zone disclosure requirement.

questions.question_text: A six-unit apartment building is located in a FEMA flood zone. Does RSA 477:4-d require the seller to disclose this?
questions.correct_answer: No, it only applies to 1-4 units
distractors: Yes, if any unit is residential
Only if flood insurance is active
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-d requires flood zone disclosure only for 1-4 family residential properties. A six-unit apartment building exceeds this threshold and is not subject to this specific disclosure statute.

questions.question_text: A seller of a single-family home in a FEMA flood zone asks their agent whether flood zone disclosure is required. What is the correct answer?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, RSA 477:4-d requires flood zone disclosure
distractors: No, flood zone disclosure is only voluntary
Only if the buyer requests it in writing
Only if the property has active flood insurance
questions.explanation: RSA 477:4-d requires sellers of 1-4 family residential properties to disclose whether the property is in a FEMA-designated flood hazard zone. This disclosure must be provided before the purchase and sale agreement.
